flow_points: Australia began with a strong Powerplay, scoring 70 runs for 0 wickets at an RR of 11.67.
MADI Hemantha then delivered a crucial bowling spell of 4.0 overs, taking 3 wickets for 37 runs.
Hemantha's spell sustained defensive pressure on the opposition with an impact score of 110.06.
Sri Lanka commenced their chase aggressively, making 61 runs for 1 wicket in their Powerplay (RR: 10.17).
P Nissanka emerged as the central figure, anchoring Sri Lanka's innings.
Nissanka reached a century, scoring 100 runs off 55 balls.
His dominant innings, contributing 54.3% of the total runs, was a major turning point for Sri Lanka.
Sri Lanka finished their innings with a high-scoring run rate of 14.33 in the Death Overs.
This powerful finish in the Death Overs decisively shifted the match momentum.
Sri Lanka clinched the victory, winning the match by 8 wickets.
